LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DAWN THOMPSON, ANTHONY JAO, AJ'S HARDWOOD FLOORING, INC'S MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: THE COURT RECONSIDERS ITS TENTATIVE RULING IN LIGHT OF PRINCE V. UNITED NATIONAL INSURANCE CO. (2006) 142 CAL.APP.4TH 233, 242. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR A NEW TRIAL IS DENIED. THE COURT'S ORIGINAL DECISION ON SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS BASED ON INTERPRETATION AND APPLICATION OF CALIFORNIA LAW. THE COURT CANNOT FOLLOW OUT-OF-STATE COURTS' INTERPRETATION OF LIKE INSURANCE CLAUSES TO FIND THAT ITS EARLIER RULING WAS AN ERROR IN LAW PER C.C.P. 657(7). COUNSEL NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE. ORDER SIGNED.
NOTICE OF APPEAL/REQUEST FOR TRANSCRIPT (NTC OF APPEAL ONLY) FILED BY PLAINTIFF THOMPSON, DAWN M JAO, ANTHONY AJ'S HARDWOOD FLOORING, INC

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BANKERS INSURANCE COMPANY, BANKERS INSURANCE GROUP, BANKERS FINANCIAL CORPORATION DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. ALTHOUGH A COPY OF THE CONTRACT NEED NOT BE ATTACHED TO THE COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD THE ESSENTIAL TERMS OF THE CONTRACT. ALSO, UNCLEAR WHETHER ONE OR MORE CONTRACTS ARE INVOLVED. ASSIGNMENT OF RIGHTS ALLEGATION IS SUFFICIENTLY PLED. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Plaintiff Dawn Thompson, Anthony Jao, Aj'S Hardwood Flooring, Inc Notice Of Hearing On Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar Aug-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF DAWN THOMPSON, ANTHONY JAO, AJ'S HARDWOOD FLOORING, INC'S MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL.
Appearances: Jeffrey S. Kaplan of the Law Offices of Gaglione Dolan & Kaplan (310.914.7966) for defendants and responding parties; Gerald Clausen of the Law Office of Gerald Clausen (391.4475) and Demian I. Oksenendler of Mannion & Lowe (733.1054) for plaintiffs and moving parties.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
---
After hearing, the court ruled as follows: the court reconsiders its tentative ruling in light of Prince v. United National Insurance Co. (2006) 142 Cal.App.4th 233, 242. Plaintiffs' motion for a new trial is denied. The court's original decision on summary judgment was based on interpretation and application of California law. The court cannot follow out-of-state courts' interpretation of like insurance clauses to find that its earlier ruling was an error in law per C.C.P. 657(7). Counsel notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Order signed.

Notice Of Hearing On Motion For New Trial
Off Calendar Aug-11-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS DAWN THOMPSON, ANTHONY JAO, AJ'S HARDWOOD FLOORING, INC'S MOTION FOR NEW TRIAL.
Appearances: Jeffrey S. Kaplan of the Law Offices of Gaglione Dolan & Kaplan (310.914.7966) for defendants and responding parties; no other appearances.
Mr. Kaplan did not give notice of his intent to contest the tentative ruling. The court granted his request for a continuance of the matter to August 12, 2011 for argument. Mr. Kaplan to give notice. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS BANKERS INSURANCE COMPANY, BANKERS INSURANCE GROUP, BANKERS FINANCIAL CORPORATION DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Craig D. Aronson, Esq. of Gaglione Dolan & Kaplan (310) 914-7966 Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendants and Moving party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Although a copy of the contract need not be attached to the complaint, plaintiff must plead the essential terms of the contract. Also, unclear whether one or more contracts are involved. Assignment of rights allegation is sufficiently pled. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is granted, 10 days leave to amend to plead punitive damages with greater specificity. (Moving party to prepare a form of order.)
